NKJV.1: Let as many bondservants as are under the yoke count their own masters worthy of all honor, so that the name of God and His doctrine may not be blasphemed.
NKJV.2: And those who have believing masters, let them not despise them because they are brethren, but rather serve them because those who are benefited are believers and beloved. Teach and exhort these things.
NKJV.3: If anyone teaches otherwise and does not consent to wholesome words, even the words of our Lord Jesus Christ, and to the doctrine which accords with godliness,
NKJV.4: he is proud, knowing nothing, but is obsessed with disputes and arguments over words, from which come envy, strife, reviling, evil suspicions,
NKJV.5: useless wranglings of men of corrupt minds and destitute of the truth, who suppose that godliness is a means of gain. From such withdraw yourself.
NKJV.6: Now godliness with contentment is great gain.
NKJV.7: For we brought nothing into this world, and it is certain we can carry nothing out.
NKJV.8: And having food and clothing, with these we shall be content.
NKJV.9: But those who desire to be rich fall into temptation and a snare, and into many foolish and harmful lusts which drown men in destruction and perdition.
NKJV.10: For the love of money is a root of all kinds of evil, for which some have strayed from the faith in their greediness, and pierced themselves through with many sorrows.
NKJV.11: But you, O man of God, flee these things and pursue righteousness, godliness, faith, love, patience, gentleness.
NKJV.12: Fight the good fight of faith, lay hold on eternal life, to which you were also called and have confessed the good confession in the presence of many witnesses.
NKJV.13: I urge you in the sight of God who gives life to all things, and before Christ Jesus who witnessed the good confession before Pontius Pilate,
NKJV.14: that you keep this commandment without spot, blameless until our Lord Jesus Christ’s appearing,
NKJV.15: which He will manifest in His own time, He who is the blessed and only Potentate, the King of kings and Lord of lords,
NKJV.16: who alone has immortality, dwelling in unapproachable light, whom no man has seen or can see, to whom be honor and everlasting power. Amen.
NKJV.17: Command those who are rich in this present age not to be haughty, nor to trust in uncertain riches but in the living God, who gives us richly all things to enjoy.
NKJV.18: Let them do good, that they be rich in good works, ready to give, willing to share,
NKJV.19: storing up for themselves a good foundation for the time to come, that they may lay hold on eternal life.
NKJV.20: O Timothy! Guard what was committed to your trust, avoiding the profane and idle babblings and contradictions of what is falsely called knowledge—
NKJV.21: by professing it some have strayed concerning the faith. Grace be with you. Amen.

ESV.1: Let all who are under a yoke as bondservants  regard their own masters as worthy of all honor, so that the name of God and the teaching may not be reviled.
ESV.2: Those who have believing masters must not be disrespectful on the ground that they are brothers; rather they must serve all the better since those who benefit by their good service are believers and beloved. False Teachers and True Contentment Teach and urge these things.
ESV.3: If anyone teaches a different doctrine and does not agree with the sound  words of our Lord Jesus Christ and the teaching that accords with godliness,
ESV.4: he is puffed up with conceit and understands nothing. He has an unhealthy craving for controversy and for quarrels about words, which produce envy, dissension, slander, evil suspicions,
ESV.5: and constant friction among people who are depraved in mind and deprived of the truth, imagining that godliness is a means of gain.
ESV.6: But godliness with contentment is great gain,
ESV.7: for we brought nothing into the world, and  we cannot take anything out of the world.
ESV.8: But if we have food and clothing, with these we will be content.
ESV.9: But those who desire to be rich fall into temptation, into a snare, into many senseless and harmful desires that plunge people into ruin and destruction.
ESV.10: For the love of money is a root of all kinds of evils. It is through this craving that some have wandered away from the faith and pierced themselves with many pangs.
ESV.11: But as for you, O man of God, flee these things. Pursue righteousness, godliness, faith, love, steadfastness, gentleness.
ESV.12: Fight the good fight of the faith. Take hold of the eternal life to which you were called and about which you made the good confession in the presence of many witnesses.
ESV.13: I charge you in the presence of God, who gives life to all things, and of Christ Jesus, who in his testimony before  Pontius Pilate made the good confession,
ESV.14: to keep the commandment unstained and free from reproach until the appearing of our Lord Jesus Christ,
ESV.15: which he will display at the proper time — he who is the blessed and only Sovereign, the King of kings and Lord of lords,
ESV.16: who alone has immortality, who dwells in unapproachable light, whom no one has ever seen or can see. To him be honor and eternal dominion. Amen.
ESV.17: As for the rich in this present age, charge them not to be haughty, nor to set their hopes on the uncertainty of riches, but on God, who richly provides us with everything to enjoy.
ESV.18: They are to do good, to be rich in good works, to be generous and ready to share,
ESV.19: thus storing up treasure for themselves as a good foundation for the future, so that they may take hold of that which is truly life.
ESV.20: O Timothy, guard the deposit entrusted to you. Avoid the irreverent babble and contradictions of what is falsely called “knowledge,”
ESV.21: for by professing it some have swerved from the faith. Grace be with you.

NASB.1: All who are under the yoke as slaves are to regard their own masters as worthy of all honor so that the name of God and [our] doctrine will not be spoken against.
NASB.2: Those who have believers as their masters must not be disrespectful to them because they are brethren, but must serve them all the more, because those who partake of the benefit are believers and beloved. Teach and preach these [principles].
NASB.3: If anyone advocates a different doctrine and does not agree with sound words, those of our Lord Jesus Christ, and with the doctrine conforming to godliness,
NASB.4: he is conceited [and] understands nothing; but he has a morbid interest in controversial questions and disputes about words, out of which arise envy, strife, abusive language, evil suspicions,
NASB.5: and constant friction between men of depraved mind and deprived of the truth, who suppose that godliness is a means of gain.
NASB.6: But godliness [actually] is a means of great gain when accompanied by contentment.
NASB.7: For we have brought nothing into the world, so we cannot take anything out of it either.
NASB.8: If we have food and covering, with these we shall be content.
NASB.9: But those who want to get rich fall into temptation and a snare and many foolish and harmful desires which plunge men into ruin and destruction.
NASB.10: For the love of money is a root of all sorts of evil, and some by longing for it have wandered away from the faith and pierced themselves with many griefs.
NASB.11: But flee from these things, you man of God, and pursue righteousness, godliness, faith, love, perseverance [and] gentleness.
NASB.12: Fight the good fight of faith; take hold of the eternal life to which you were called, and you made the good confession in the presence of many witnesses.
NASB.13: I charge you in the presence of God, who gives life to all things, and of Christ Jesus, who testified the good confession before Pontius Pilate,
NASB.14: that you keep the commandment without stain or reproach until the appearing of our Lord Jesus Christ,
NASB.15: which He will bring about at the proper time-- He who is the blessed and only Sovereign, the King of kings and Lord of lords,
NASB.16: who alone possesses immortality and dwells in unapproachable light, whom no man has seen or can see. To Him [be] honor and eternal dominion! Amen.
NASB.17: Instruct those who are rich in this present world not to be conceited or to fix their hope on the uncertainty of riches, but on God, who richly supplies us with all things to enjoy.
NASB.18: [Instruct them] to do good, to be rich in good works, to be generous and ready to share,
NASB.19: storing up for themselves the treasure of a good foundation for the future, so that they may take hold of that which is life indeed.
NASB.20: O Timothy, guard what has been entrusted to you, avoiding worldly [and] empty chatter [and] the opposing arguments of what is falsely called 'knowledge '--
NASB.21: which some have professed and thus gone astray from the faith. Grace be with you.

NLT.1: All slaves should show full respect for their masters so they will not bring shame on the name of God and his teaching.
NLT.2: If the masters are believers, that is no excuse for being disrespectful. Those slaves should work all the harder because their efforts are helping other believers who are well loved. False Teaching and True Riches Teach these things, Timothy, and encourage everyone to obey them.
NLT.3: Some people may contradict our teaching, but these are the wholesome teachings of the Lord Jesus Christ. These teachings promote a godly life.
NLT.4: Anyone who teaches something different is arrogant and lacks understanding. Such a person has an unhealthy desire to quibble over the meaning of words. This stirs up arguments ending in jealousy, division, slander, and evil suspicions.
NLT.5: These people always cause trouble. Their minds are corrupt, and they have turned their backs on the truth. To them, a show of godliness is just a way to become wealthy.
NLT.6: Yet true godliness with contentment is itself great wealth.
NLT.7: After all, we brought nothing with us when we came into the world, and we can’t take anything with us when we leave it.
NLT.8: So if we have enough food and clothing, let us be content.
NLT.9: But people who long to be rich fall into temptation and are trapped by many foolish and harmful desires that plunge them into ruin and destruction.
NLT.10: For the love of money is the root of all kinds of evil. And some people, craving money, have wandered from the true faith and pierced themselves with many sorrows.
NLT.11: But you, Timothy, are a man of God; so run from all these evil things. Pursue righteousness and a godly life, along with faith, love, perseverance, and gentleness.
NLT.12: Fight the good fight for the true faith. Hold tightly to the eternal life to which God has called you, which you have declared so well before many witnesses.
NLT.13: And I charge you before God, who gives life to all, and before Christ Jesus, who gave a good testimony before Pontius Pilate,
NLT.14: that you obey this command without wavering. Then no one can find fault with you from now until our Lord Jesus Christ comes again.
NLT.15: For, At just the right time Christ will be revealed from heaven by the blessed and only almighty God, the King of all kings and Lord of all lords.
NLT.16: He alone can never die, and he lives in light so brilliant that no human can approach him. No human eye has ever seen him, nor ever will. All honor and power to him forever! Amen.
NLT.17: Teach those who are rich in this world not to be proud and not to trust in their money, which is so unreliable. Their trust should be in God, who richly gives us all we need for our enjoyment.
NLT.18: Tell them to use their money to do good. They should be rich in good works and generous to those in need, always being ready to share with others.
NLT.19: By doing this they will be storing up their treasure as a good foundation for the future so that they may experience true life.
NLT.20: Timothy, guard what God has entrusted to you. Avoid godless, foolish discussions with those who oppose you with their so-called knowledge.
NLT.21: Some people have wandered from the faith by following such foolishness. May God’s grace be with you all.

WEB.1: Let as many as are bondservants under the yoke count their own masters worthy of all honor, that the name of God and the doctrine not be blasphemed.
WEB.2: Those who have believing masters, let them not despise them, because they are brothers, but rather let them serve them, because those who partake of the benefit are believing and beloved. Teach and exhort these things.
WEB.3: If anyone teaches a different doctrine, and doesn’t consent to sound words, the words of our Lord Jesus Christ, and to the doctrine which is according to godliness,
WEB.4: he is conceited, knowing nothing, but obsessed with arguments, disputes, and word battles, from which come envy, strife, insulting, evil suspicions,
WEB.5: constant friction of people of corrupt minds and destitute of the truth, who suppose that godliness is a means of gain. Withdraw yourself from such.
WEB.6: But godliness with contentment is great gain.
WEB.7: For we brought nothing into the world, and we certainly can’t carry anything out.
WEB.8: But having food and clothing, we will be content with that.
WEB.9: But those who are determined to be rich fall into a temptation and a snare and many foolish and harmful lusts, such as drown men in ruin and destruction.
WEB.10: For the love of money is a root of all kinds of evil. Some have been led astray from the faith in their greed, and have pierced themselves through with many sorrows.
WEB.11: But you, man of God, flee these things, and follow after righteousness, godliness, faith, love, perseverance, and gentleness.
WEB.12: Fight the good fight of faith. Take hold of the eternal life to which you were called, and you confessed the good confession in the sight of many witnesses.
WEB.13: I command you before God, who gives life to all things, and before Christ Jesus, who before Pontius Pilate testified the good confession,
WEB.14: that you keep the commandment without spot, blameless, until the appearing of our Lord Jesus Christ;
WEB.15: which in its own times he will show, who is the blessed and only Ruler, the King of kings, and Lord of lords;
WEB.16: who alone has immortality, dwelling in unapproachable light; whom no man has seen, nor can see: to whom be honor and eternal power. Amen.
WEB.17: Charge those who are rich in this present world that they not be haughty, nor have their hope set on the uncertainty of riches, but on the living God, who richly provides us with everything to enjoy;
WEB.18: that they do good, that they be rich in good works, that they be ready to distribute, willing to communicate;
WEB.19: laying up in store for themselves a good foundation against the time to come, that they may lay hold of eternal life.
WEB.20: Timothy, guard that which is committed to you, turning away from the empty chatter and oppositions of what is falsely called knowledge;
WEB.21: which some profess, and thus have wandered from the faith. Grace be with you. Amen.

CSB17.1: All who are under the yoke as slaves should regard their own masters <sup>[1]</sup> as worthy of all respect, so that God’s name <sup>ⓒ</sup> and his teaching will not be blasphemed.
CSB17.2: Let those who have believing masters not be disrespectful to them because they are brothers, but serve them even better, since those who benefit from their service are believers and dearly loved. <sup>[2]</sup> <sup>ⓓ</sup> Teach and encourage these things.
CSB17.3: If anyone teaches false doctrine and does not agree with the sound teaching of our Lord <sup>ⓔ</sup> Jesus Christ and with the teaching that promotes godliness, <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.4: he is conceited and understands nothing, but has an unhealthy interest in disputes and arguments over words. <sup>ⓖ</sup> From these come envy, <sup>ⓗ</sup> quarreling, <sup>ⓘ</sup> slander, <sup>ⓙ</sup> evil suspicions,
CSB17.5: and constant disagreement among people whose minds are depraved and deprived of the truth, who imagine that godliness is a way to material gain. <sup>[3]</sup> <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.6: But godliness with contentment is great gain. <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.7: For we brought nothing into the world, and <sup>[4]</sup> we can take nothing out. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.8: If we have food and clothing, <sup>[5]</sup> we will be content with these.
CSB17.9: But those who want to be rich fall into temptation, a trap, and many foolish and harmful desires, which plunge people into ruin and destruction.
CSB17.10: For the love of money is a root <sup>[6]</sup> of all kinds of evil, and by craving it, some have wandered away from the faith <sup>ⓝ</sup> and pierced themselves with many griefs.
CSB17.11: But you, man of God, flee from these things, and pursue righteousness, godliness, faith, love, <sup>ⓐ</sup> endurance, <sup>ⓑ</sup> and gentleness.
CSB17.12: Fight the good fight <sup>ⓒ</sup> of the faith. Take hold of eternal life <sup>ⓓ</sup> to which you were called and about which you have made a good confession in the presence of many witnesses.
CSB17.13: In the presence of God, who gives life to all, <sup>ⓔ</sup> and of Christ Jesus, who gave a good confession before Pontius Pilate, <sup>ⓕ</sup> I charge you
CSB17.14: to keep this command without fault or failure until the appearing <sup>ⓖ</sup> of our Lord <sup>ⓗ</sup> Jesus Christ.
CSB17.15: God will bring this about in his own time. <sup>ⓘ</sup> He is the blessed and only <sup>ⓙ</sup> Sovereign, the King of kings, <sup>ⓚ</sup> and the Lord of lords, <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.16: who alone is immortal and who lives in unapproachable light, <sup>ⓜ</sup> whom no one has seen or can see, <sup>ⓝ</sup> to him be honor and eternal power. <sup>ⓐ</sup> Amen. <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.17: Instruct those who are rich in the present age not to be arrogant <sup>ⓒ</sup> or to set their hope on the uncertainty of wealth, but on God, <sup>[7]</sup> who richly provides us with all things <sup>ⓓ</sup> to enjoy.
CSB17.18: Instruct them to do what is good, to be rich in good works, <sup>ⓔ</sup> to be generous and willing to share, <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.19: storing up treasure for themselves as a good foundation for the coming age, <sup>ⓖ</sup> so that they may take hold of what is truly life.
CSB17.20: Timothy, guard what has been entrusted to you, <sup>ⓗ</sup> avoiding <sup>ⓘ</sup> irreverent and empty speech and contradictions from what is falsely called knowledge. <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.21: By professing it, some <sup>ⓚ</sup> people have departed from the faith. <sup>ⓛ</sup> Grace be with you all.

AMP.1: All who are under the yoke as bond-servants (slaves) are to regard their own masters as worthy of honor and respect so that the name of God and the teaching [about Him] will not be spoken against.
AMP.2: Those who have believing masters are not to be disrespectful toward them because they are brothers [in Christ], but they should serve them even better, because those who benefit from their kindly service are believers and beloved. Teach and urge these [duties and principles].
AMP.3: If anyone teaches a different doctrine and does not agree with the sound words of our Lord Jesus Christ, and with the doctrine and teaching which is in agreement with godliness (personal integrity, upright behavior),
AMP.4: he is conceited and woefully ignorant [understanding nothing]. He has a morbid interest in controversial questions and disputes about words, which produces envy, quarrels, verbal abuse, evil suspicions,
AMP.5: and perpetual friction between men who are corrupted in mind and deprived of the truth, who think that godliness is a source of profit [a lucrative, money-making business—withdraw from them].
AMP.6: But godliness actually is a source of great gain when accompanied by contentment [that contentment which comes from a sense of inner confidence based on the sufficiency of God].
AMP.7: For we have brought nothing into the world, so [it is clear that] we cannot take anything out of it, either.
AMP.8: But if we have food and clothing, with these we will be content.
AMP.9: But those who [are not financially ethical and] crave to get rich [with a compulsive, greedy longing for wealth] fall into temptation and a trap and into many foolish and harmful desires that plunge people into ruin and destruction [leading to personal misery].
AMP.10: For the love of money [that is, the greedy desire for it and the willingness to gain it unethically] is a root of all sorts of evil, and some by longing for it have wandered away from the faith and pierced themselves [through and through] with many sorrows.
AMP.11: But as for you, O man of God, flee from these things; aim at and pursue righteousness [true goodness, moral conformity to the character of God], godliness [the fear of God], faith, love, steadfastness, and gentleness.
AMP.12: Fight the good fight of the faith [in the conflict with evil]; take hold of the eternal life to which you were called, and [for which] you made the good confession [of faith] in the presence of many witnesses.
AMP.13: I solemnly charge you in the presence of God, who gives life to all things, and [in the presence] of Christ Jesus, who made the good confession [in His testimony] before Pontius Pilate,
AMP.14: to keep all His precepts without stain or reproach until the appearing of our Lord Jesus Christ,
AMP.15: which He will bring about in His own time—He who is the blessed and only Sovereign [the absolute Ruler], the King of those who reign as kings and Lord of those who rule as lords,
AMP.16: He alone possesses immortality [absolute exemption from death] and lives in unapproachable light, whom no man has ever seen or can see. To Him be honor and eternal power and dominion! Amen.
AMP.17: As for the rich in this present world, instruct them not to be conceited and arrogant, nor to set their hope on the uncertainty of riches, but on God, who richly and ceaselessly provides us with everything for our enjoyment.
AMP.18: Instruct them to do good, to be rich in good works, to be generous, willing to share [with others].
AMP.19: In this way storing up for themselves the enduring riches of a good foundation for the future, so that they may take hold of that which is truly life.
AMP.20: O Timothy, guard and keep safe the deposit [of godly truth] entrusted to you, turn away from worldly and godless chatter [with its profane, empty words], and the contradictions of what is falsely called "knowledge"—
AMP.21:
which some have professed and by doing so have erred (missed the mark) and strayed from the faith.
Grace be with you.

MSG.1: Whoever is a slave must make the best of it, giving respect to his master so that outsiders don't blame God and our teaching for his behavior.
MSG.2: Slaves with Christian masters all the more so—their masters are really their beloved brothers! These are the things I want you to teach and preach.
MSG.3: If you have leaders there who teach otherwise, who refuse the solid words of our Master Jesus and this godly instruction,
MSG.4: tag them for what they are: ignorant windbags who infect the air with germs of envy, controversy, bad-mouthing, suspicious rumors.
MSG.5: Eventually there's an epidemic of backstabbing, and truth is but a distant memory. They think religion is a way to make a fast buck.
MSG.6: A devout life does bring wealth, but it's the rich simplicity of being yourself before God.
MSG.7: Since we entered the world penniless and will leave it penniless,
MSG.8: if we have bread on the table and shoes on our feet, that's enough.
MSG.9: But if it's only money these leaders are after, they'll self-destruct in no time.
MSG.10: Lust for money brings trouble and nothing but trouble. Going down that path, some lose their footing in the faith completely and live to regret it bitterly ever after.
MSG.11: But you, Timothy, man of God: Run for your life from all this. Pursue a righteous life—a life of wonder, faith, love, steadiness, courtesy.
MSG.12: Run hard and fast in the faith. Seize the eternal life, the life you were called to, the life you so fervently embraced in the presence of so many witnesses.
MSG.13: I'm charging you before the life-giving God and before Christ, who took his stand before Pontius Pilate and didn't give an inch:
MSG.14: Keep this command to the letter, and don't slack off. Our Master, Jesus Christ, is on his way.
MSG.15: He'll show up right on time, his arrival guaranteed by the Blessed and Undisputed Ruler, High King, High God.
MSG.16: He's the only one death can't touch, his light so bright no one can get close. He's never been seen by human eyes—human eyes can't take him in! Honor to him, and eternal rule! Oh, yes.
MSG.17: Tell those rich in this world's wealth to quit being so full of themselves and so obsessed with money, which is here today and gone tomorrow. Tell them to go after God, who piles on all the riches we could ever manage—
MSG.18: to do good, to be rich in helping others, to be extravagantly generous.
MSG.19: If they do that, they'll build a treasury that will last, gaining life that is truly life.
MSG.20: And oh, my dear Timothy, guard the treasure you were given! Guard it with your life. Avoid the talk-show religion and the practiced confusion of the so-called experts.
MSG.21: People caught up in a lot of talk can miss the whole point of faith. Overwhelming grace keep you!

NIV.1: All who are under the yoke of slavery should consider their masters worthy of full respect, so that God’s name and our teaching may not be slandered.
NIV.2: Those who have believing masters are not to show less respect for them because they are brothers. Instead, they are to serve them even better, because those who benefit from their service are believers, and dear to them. These are the things you are to teach and urge on them.
NIV.3:
Love of Money
If anyone teaches false doctrines and does not agree to the sound instruction of our Lord Jesus Christ and to godly teaching,
NIV.4: he is conceited and understands nothing. He has an unhealthy interest in controversies and quarrels about words that result in envy, strife, malicious talk, evil suspicions
NIV.5: and constant friction between men of corrupt mind, who have been robbed of the truth and who think that godliness is a means to financial gain.
NIV.6: But godliness with contentment is great gain.
NIV.7: For we brought nothing into the world, and we can take nothing out of it.
NIV.8: But if we have food and clothing, we will be content with that.
NIV.9: People who want to get rich fall into temptation and a trap and into many foolish and harmful desires that plunge men into ruin and destruction.
NIV.10: For the love of money is a root of all kinds of evil. Some people, eager for money, have wandered from the faith and pierced themselves with many griefs.
NIV.11:
Paul’s Charge to Timothy
But you, man of God, flee from all this, and pursue righteousness, godliness, faith, love, endurance and gentleness.
NIV.12: Fight the good fight of the faith. Take hold of the eternal life to which you were called when you made your good confession in the presence of many witnesses.
NIV.13: In the sight of God, who gives life to everything, and of Christ Jesus, who while testifying before Pontius Pilate made the good confession, I charge you
NIV.14: to keep this command without spot or blame until the appearing of our Lord Jesus Christ,
NIV.15: which God will bring about in his own time—God, the blessed and only Ruler, the King of kings and Lord of lords,
NIV.16: who alone is immortal and who lives in unapproachable light, whom no one has seen or can see. To him be honor and might forever. Amen.
NIV.17: Command those who are rich in this present world not to be arrogant nor to put their hope in wealth, which is so uncertain, but to put their hope in God, who richly provides us with everything for our enjoyment.
NIV.18: Command them to do good, to be rich in good deeds, and to be generous and willing to share.
NIV.19: In this way they will lay up treasure for themselves as a firm foundation for the coming age, so that they may take hold of the life that is truly life.
NIV.20: Timothy, guard what has been entrusted to your care. Turn away from godless chatter and the opposing ideas of what is falsely called knowledge,
NIV.21:
which some have professed and in so doing have wandered from the faith.
Grace be with you.

NRSVCE.1: Let all who are under the yoke of slavery regard their masters as worthy of all honor, so that the name of God and the teaching may not be blasphemed.
NRSVCE.2: Those who have believing masters must not be disrespectful to them on the ground that they are members of the church; rather they must serve them all the more, since those who benefit by their service are believers and beloved. False Teaching and True Riches Teach and urge these duties.
NRSVCE.3: Whoever teaches otherwise and does not agree with the sound words of our Lord Jesus Christ and the teaching that is in accordance with godliness,
NRSVCE.4: is conceited, understanding nothing, and has a morbid craving for controversy and for disputes about words. From these come envy, dissension, slander, base suspicions,
NRSVCE.5: and wrangling among those who are depraved in mind and bereft of the truth, imagining that godliness is a means of gain.
NRSVCE.6: Of course, there is great gain in godliness combined with contentment;
NRSVCE.7: for we brought nothing into the world, so that we can take nothing out of it;
NRSVCE.8: but if we have food and clothing, we will be content with these.
NRSVCE.9: But those who want to be rich fall into temptation and are trapped by many senseless and harmful desires that plunge people into ruin and destruction.
NRSVCE.10: For the love of money is a root of all kinds of evil, and in their eagerness to be rich some have wandered away from the faith and pierced themselves with many pains.
NRSVCE.11: But as for you, man of God, shun all this; pursue righteousness, godliness, faith, love, endurance, gentleness.
NRSVCE.12: Fight the good fight of the faith; take hold of the eternal life, to which you were called and for which you made the good confession in the presence of many witnesses.
NRSVCE.13: In the presence of God, who gives life to all things, and of Christ Jesus, who in his testimony before Pontius Pilate made the good confession, I charge you
NRSVCE.14: to keep the commandment without spot or blame until the manifestation of our Lord Jesus Christ,
NRSVCE.15: which he will bring about at the right time — he who is the blessed and only Sovereign, the King of kings and Lord of lords.
NRSVCE.16: It is he alone who has immortality and dwells in unapproachable light, whom no one has ever seen or can see; to him be honor and eternal dominion. Amen.
NRSVCE.17: As for those who in the present age are rich, command them not to be haughty, or to set their hopes on the uncertainty of riches, but rather on God who richly provides us with everything for our enjoyment.
NRSVCE.18: They are to do good, to be rich in good works, generous, and ready to share,
NRSVCE.19: thus storing up for themselves the treasure of a good foundation for the future, so that they may take hold of the life that really is life.
NRSVCE.20: Timothy, guard what has been entrusted to you. Avoid the profane chatter and contradictions of what is falsely called knowledge;
NRSVCE.21: by professing it some have missed the mark as regards the faith. Grace be with you.

NET.1: Those who are under the yoke as slaves must regard their own masters as deserving of full respect. This will prevent the name of God and Christian teaching from being discredited.
NET.2: But those who have believing masters must not show them less respect because they are brothers. Instead they are to serve all the more, because those who benefit from their service are believers and dearly loved. Teach them and exhort them about these things.
NET.3: If someone spreads false teachings and does not agree with sound words (that is, those of our Lord Jesus Christ) and with the teaching that accords with godliness,
NET.4: he is conceited and understands nothing, but has an unhealthy interest in controversies and verbal disputes. This gives rise to envy, dissension, slanders, evil suspicions,
NET.5: and constant bickering by people corrupted in their minds and deprived of the truth, who suppose that godliness is a way of making a profit.
NET.6: Now godliness combined with contentment brings great profit.
NET.7: For we have brought nothing into this world and so we cannot take a single thing out either.
NET.8: But if we have food and shelter, we will be satisfied with that.
NET.9: Those who long to be rich, however, stumble into temptation and a trap and many senseless and harmful desires that plunge people into ruin and destruction.
NET.10: For the love of money is the root of all evils. Some people in reaching for it have strayed from the faith and stabbed themselves with many pains.
NET.11: But you, as a person dedicated to God, keep away from all that. Instead pursue righteousness, godliness, faithfulness, love, endurance, and gentleness.
NET.12: Compete well for the faith and lay hold of that eternal life you were called for and made your good confession for in the presence of many witnesses.
NET.13: I charge you before God who gives life to all things and Christ Jesus who made his good confession before Pontius Pilate,
NET.14: to obey this command without fault or failure until the appearing of our Lord Jesus Christ
NET.15: — whose appearing the blessed and only Sovereign, the King of kings and Lord of lords, will reveal at the right time.
NET.16: He alone possesses immortality and lives in unapproachable light, whom no human has ever seen or is able to see. To him be honor and eternal power! Amen.
NET.17: Command those who are rich in this world’s goods not to be haughty or to set their hope on riches, which are uncertain, but on God who richly provides us with all things for our enjoyment.
NET.18: Tell them to do good, to be rich in good deeds, to be generous givers, sharing with others.
NET.19: In this way they will save up a treasure for themselves as a firm foundation for the future and so lay hold of what is truly life.
NET.20: O Timothy, protect what has been entrusted to you. Avoid the profane chatter and absurdities of so-called “knowledge.”
NET.21: By professing it, some have strayed from the faith. Grace be with you all.
